summaryText: Guest speaker Montel Jordan opened with a personal testimony about his ongoing cancer treatment, framing his message on generosity from a place of vulnerability and trust in God. He taught that the Christian life is built on the principle of 'give and it shall be given,' which applies far beyond finances to our attitudes, judgments, and love. The sermon was structured around three areas: generous living, which involves serving others and storing up treasures in heaven; generous giving, which is an act of obedience rooted in tithing and trusting God as provider; and generous loving, which flows from intimacy with God and covers offenses. Pastor Montel emphasized that these are not just principles to know but practices to implement, and that what we measure out—whether criticism, forgiveness, or love—is what will be measured back to us. He concluded with a powerful altar call, inviting those feeling distant or in bondage to experience God's generous love and salvation, framing it as an act of 'robbing hell' to reclaim what the enemy has stolen.
mainPoints: Generous living is a practice of serving and storing up treasure in heaven, based on the principle that what you measure out is measured back to you.
Generous giving is an act of obedient trust, demonstrated through tithing, which invites God to open the windows of heaven and pour out blessing.
Generous loving flows from proximity to God, covers offenses, and is not performance-based, mirroring the unconditional love Christ showed us.

versesMentioned.text: Do not judge, and you will not be judged. Do not condemn, and you will not be condemned. Forgive, and you will be forgiven. Give, and it will be given to you. A good measure, pressed down, shaken together and running over, will be poured into your lap. For with the measure you use, it will be measured to you.
Be careful not to practice your righteousness in front of others to be seen by them. If you do, you will have no reward from your Father in heaven.

Will a man rob God? Yet you rob me. But you ask, 'How do we rob you?' In tithes and offerings. You are under a curse—your whole nation—because you are robbing me. Bring the whole tithe into the storehouse, that there may be food in my house. Test me in this,' says the LORD Almighty, 'and see if I will not throw open the floodgates of heaven and pour out so much blessing that there will not be room enough to store it.
But Samuel replied: 'Does the LORD delight in burnt offerings and sacrifices as much as in obeying the LORD? To obey is better than sacrifice, and to heed is better than the fat of rams.'

Love is patient, love is kind. It does not envy, it does not boast, it is not proud. It does not dishonor others, it is not self-seeking, it is not easily angered, it keeps no record of wrongs. Love does not delight in evil but rejoices with the truth. It always protects, always trusts, always hopes, always perseveres. Love never fails.
Above all, love each other deeply, because love covers over a multitude of sins.
But God demonstrates his own love for us in this: While we were still sinners, Christ died for us.

For God so loved the world that he gave his one and only Son, that whoever believes in him shall not perish but have eternal life. For God did not send his Son into the world to condemn the world, but to save the world through him.

Monday.scripture: Luke 6:38 - 'Give, and it will be given to you. A good measure, pressed down, shaken together and running over, will be poured into your lap. For with the measure you use, it will be measured to you.'
Monday.action: Take inventory of what you are 'measuring out' today—your words, attitudes, and judgments. Intentionally choose to measure out grace and encouragement.
Monday.prayer: Lord, make me aware of what I am giving out today. Help me to be a conduit of your grace, knowing that what I sow, I will also reap.

Tuesday.scripture: Matthew 6:1 - 'Be careful not to practice your righteousness in front of others to be seen by them. If you do, you will have no reward from your Father in heaven.'
Tuesday.action: Perform one act of service or kindness today without anyone knowing. Let it be between you and God.
Tuesday.prayer: Father, help me to live generously not for recognition, but out of love for you. Let my secret acts of kindness store up treasure in heaven.

Wednesday.scripture: Malachi 3:10 - 'Bring the whole tithe into the storehouse... Test me in this,' says the LORD Almighty, 'and see if I will not throw open the floodgates of heaven and pour out so much blessing that there will not be room enough to store it.'
Wednesday.action: Review your finances. If you are a tither, thank God for his faithfulness. If not, prayerfully take a step of obedience in your giving.
Wednesday.prayer: God, I trust you as my provider. I choose to be obedient with my finances, believing you will open the windows of heaven in my life.

Thursday.scripture: 1 Samuel 15:22 - 'To obey is better than sacrifice, and to heed is better than the fat of rams.'
Thursday.action: Identify one area where you are doing a 'good thing' but may be avoiding the 'God thing.' Commit to aligning your actions with God's clear instruction.
Thursday.prayer: Lord, I desire your approval more than impressive activity. Give me the courage to obey you fully, even when it's difficult or doesn't make sense to others.

Friday.scripture: 1 Corinthians 13:4-5 - 'Love is patient, love is kind. It does not envy, it does not boast, it is not proud. It does not dishonor others, it is not self-seeking, it is not easily angered, it keeps no record of wrongs.'
Friday.action: Think of one person you may be withholding love from due to an offense. Take a practical step to extend love to them today, whether through prayer, a kind word, or an act of service.
Friday.prayer: Jesus, your love for me was unconditional. Help me to love others the same way, to cover offenses and not keep a record of wrongs. Pour your love through me.

Saturday.scripture: Romans 5:8 - 'But God demonstrates his own love for us in this: While we were still sinners, Christ died for us.'
Saturday.action: Spend time in quiet reflection, receiving God's love for you. Write down one area where you feel unlovable and meditate on the truth that Christ died for you while you were in that state.
Saturday.prayer: God, thank you for your scandalously generous love. I receive it afresh today. Let this unconditional love heal my wounds and empower me to love others freely.
